codeReview.summary: The launch wakeup change looks directionally correct, but the onboarding failure gate is incomplete: after a failed codex_local environment check, the wizard still lets operators jump to later steps via the clickable progress tabs and see the launch screen, so failed validation does not truly block progression.

issues.description: The progress-tab buttons always call `setStep(s)` with no prerequisite checks, so after a failed adapter validation the operator can still click into Task/Launch and reach the `Ready to launch` screen even though no agent was created. That violates the feature requirement and VAL-DEMO-005 wording that failed validation must block onboarding progression and avoid an apparently successful setup state. The later guards at `handleStep3Next`/`handleLaunch` only no-op silently (`ui/src/components/OnboardingWizard.tsx:547`, `ui/src/components/OnboardingWizard.tsx:553`), which means the UI still advances into misleading success-looking steps instead of actually blocking progression. The new tests only cover the Next-button path (`ui/src/components/OnboardingWizard.test.tsx:209`, `ui/src/components/OnboardingWizard.test.tsx:232`) and would not catch this bypass.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `web-runtime-worker` procedure should explicitly allow pairing deterministic UI stubs with a direct `/adapters/:type/test-environment` API check when local adapter probes are too slow for browser automation. The worker followed this pattern and marked `followedProcedure` false even though it still gathered valid UI + readiness evidence.

summary: Fail. The feature hardens the Next-button path and adds launch wakeup, but failed codex validation is still bypassable through the onboarding progress tabs, so the UI can advance into a misleading launch state. Shared-state note: the web-runtime skill should document when deterministic browser stubs plus direct readiness API checks are acceptable evidence.
